nameMeanings.name: Mears
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Mear , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: from Ó Meidhir ‘descendant of Meidhir’, a personal name based on meidhir ‘mirth’. Compare Myers .

variants.name: Meers
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Staffordshire) and Irish (Mayo): variant of   Mears  .
Flemish and Dutch: topographic name from meers(ch) denoting lush, alluvial land by a watercourse.

variants.name: Miears
meanings: Altered form of   Mears   or   Miers  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: Mear, a field; also from mear or mearh, a horse

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
In 1781, Maryland donated land to be used for part of Washington, D. C.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mears was born in 1764, in Dorchester, Maryland, United States. She married John Cartwright about 1790, in Guilford, North Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1828, in Indiana, United States, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mears est née en 1764, à Dorchester, Maryland, États-Unis. Elle a épousé John Cartwright environ 1790, à Guilford, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1828, à Indiana, États-Unis, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mears wurde 1764 in Dorchester, Maryland, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Sie hat um 1790 in Guilford, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten John Cartwright geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1828 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Indiana, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mearsは1764年にアメリカ合衆国メリーランド州ドールチェスターで生まれた。約1790年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州グイルフォードでJohn Cartwrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1828年にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州で64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mears(은)는 1764년 미국 메릴랜드 주 도르체스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1790년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 길퍼드에서 John Cartwright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1828년 미국 인디애나 주에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mears родилась в 1764 г. в местности дорчестир, Мэриленд, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cartwright около 1790 г. в местности гуилфорд, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1828 г. в местности Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 64 лет.
